The Story Behind the Index

I presented the compute pricing framework to Goldman Sachs — MD and VP of Futures Sales Trading. They loved it. Took their feedback and kept building.

The deeper I went, the clearer it became: this needs to be the independent standard — the WTI for compute — that every institution licenses.

So I made a decision: take matters into my own hands. Built the data aggregator. Filed the patent. Incorporated the company.

Goldman validated the demand. I built the supply. Now I'm building the company.

The Problem

No standard. No visibility. No hedge.
Three unsolved problems in the world's fastest-growing commodity market.

No Pricing Standard

  • ~50 providers, ~60 chip models — no shared benchmark
  • H100 ≠ MI300X at identical $/hr — apples vs. oranges
  • Training and inference have different cost physics — no index captures both

Degradation Is Invisible

  • GPU performance decays over chip lifetime — nobody tracks it
  • Buyers pay spec-sheet prices for hardware running below spec
  • No market publishes performance curves — the data doesn't exist

Exposure Can't Be Hedged

  • $100B+/yr in compute spend locked into EDPs, MACCs, CUDs
  • Prices move — no futures, no OTC swap, no ETF to offset risk
  • Without a standard index, no instrument can exist
The Solution

The Compute Unit Index.
The WTI for GPU compute.

YCU — COMPOSITE

Harmonic mean of YTCU + YICU. The headline benchmark.

YTCU — TRAINING

Compute-bound. FP weight > bandwidth. Physics-correct.

YICU — INFERENCE

Bandwidth-bound. BW weight dominates. Physics-correct.

WTI
→
CU

WTI grades every barrel of oil.
CU grades every compute-hour.

This is running right now.

IP Protection

The methodology is patent pending.
The moat is real.

  • Non-provisional filed — USPTO review active · Track One (~9 months)
  • Core algorithm protected — geometric mean normalization, physics-calibrated 4-parameter scoring
  • Empirically verified — TCU / ICU benchmarked from actual compute jobs, not manufacturer spec sheets. Others collect specs. We measure reality.
  • Can't replicate without licensing — values published, method protected

$45M validates the market.
No one has the normalization layer.

Architect needs us. Silicon Data needs us. Ornn needs us.
Business Model

Cash flow positive before exchange listing.
Exchange is the upside, not the plan.

01 Month 1–5
Data Subscriptions
$3K/mo · $10K/mo
$3K — 200+ neocloud providers & compute brokers. Price transparency, index benchmarking. Low-friction $36K/yr buy.
$10K — Quant & prop desks (Millennium pods, Citadel, Two Sigma). Real-time GPU pricing as alt data signal for NVDA/cloud equity models.
Same model as satellite tanker imagery for oil supply — but for AI infrastructure. 200+ addressable quant buyers.
02 Month 6–12
Data Licensing
Bloomberg BDE → CU / TCU / ICU headline tickers on 325K terminals
Optional per-provider & historical data add-on

03 Month 15+
Financial Products
OTC settlement — 30–40 bps early · 20–25 · 10–15 · ~5 bps at scale
No alternative index = captive pricing · bps compress as volume multiplies

How It Works

A financial overlay on physical compute contracts.

PHYSICAL LAYER — UNCHANGED

Enterprise keeps their existing cloud contract intact.

AWS EDP — Enterprise Discount Program
Azure MACC — Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment
GCP CUD — Committed Use Discounts
+ INDEPENDENT FINANCIAL OVERLAY
CU SWAP — CONTRACT FOR DIFFERENCES

Enterprise pays: Fixed CU rate

Counterparty pays: Floating CU Index, monthly

Settlement: Net cash difference — no GPU changes hands

Exit: Sell the position. No penalties. No ratchets.

TWO-SIDED MARKET
BUYS FIXED
Cloud Providers · Hardware Owners
Revenue tied to spot prices.
Swap pays them when spot drops.
BANK EARNS SPREAD
SELLS FIXED
AI Labs · Enterprises
Costs exposed to spot spikes.
Swap pays them when spot rises, locking in prices.

Same structure as energy VPPAs.
Google, Microsoft, and Amazon already use these for electricity.

Vision

Every compute transaction in the world prices against this index.

I Now → Series A
OTC Market
30–40 bps · novel market

Bilateral ISDA swaps. Bloomberg tickers live. Prove demand — build the history CME needs.

II Series A → B
CME Listing
$0.05–$0.10 / contract

CU / TCU / ICU futures on CME. CME Clearing handles DCO. CF Benchmarks → BRR precedent.

III Series C
Yggdrasil Clearing
Index + clearing fees

CFTC-registered DCO. Full stack — index, exchange standard, clearing rail. VIX model: $100M+/yr.

IV Long-term
Asia Expansion
Second standard

~$1T unhedged compute behind the firewall. No standardized pricing exists. A generational second market.

Data Subscriptions — Running the entire way through every act. Cash flow from Day 1, compounding with brand recognition at every stage. $45–90K MRR → $150K+ → scales with exchange volume

CF Benchmarks built the Bitcoin benchmark. CME licensed it. Kraken paid $100M to own it.
We're building the compute equivalent — for a market 10× larger.
